steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with an introductory prepositional phrase: 'With almost two millennia of climate data in its tree [rings]'. The blank must correctly punctuate the boundary between that introductory phrase and the main clause that begins 'a single tree like this'.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rings and a': uses 'and' to join the phrase to the main clause as if they are parallel items, which is grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'rings, a': places a comma after the introductory phrase, correctly separating it from the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — 'rings. A': creates a period, making the introductory phrase a sentence fragment on its own. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rings; a': a semicolon must join two independent clauses. The introductory phrase is not an independent clause, so the semicolon is incorrect. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
